subject: Ecology
title: Climate Change and Islands
description: A 60-second lesson on Climate Change and Islands.

hook.text: Rising seas threaten low-lying islands—and every species that exists only on them.

buildup.text: Atolls barely a meter above sea level face saltwater intrusion, erosion, and total submersion.

discovery.text: Island species can't migrate uphill if the island is flat—there is no higher ground.

twist.text: Warmer seas also fuel stronger cyclones that strip vegetation from exposed islands.

climax.text: Some island nations are already planning relocations for both people and wildlife.

punchline.text: The ocean that made islands could also erase them.

quiz.question: Why is sea level rise especially dangerous for atoll species?
options: Flat islands have no higher ground to escape to
Atolls are too tall for waves
Species on atolls can all swim
